page: Microsoft Azure E8-4as_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EASv5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments.

Benchmark data reveals a contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The AMD EPYC processor achieves strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks, placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core CPU performance is weak, falling into the bottom 25%, which is also reflected in its weak Geekbench multi-core score of 3636.0 and Passmark CPU score of 6105.29. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance for large-block read operations at 39.23 GB/sec, but write operations and small-block cached reads are weak. Cryptographic performance is average, with a Passmark CPU encryption score of 4290.51, supported by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA instructions.

The primary tradeoff of the E8-4as_v5 is its high memory capacity relative to its limited computational throughput. Multi-threaded workloads, such as bzip3 decompression, score in the bottom 10%, and database operations are weak at 1721.86 operations in Passmark. Consequently, this server is less suited for compute-heavy parallel processing or high-throughput web serving. It is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-bound workloads that do not require intensive CPU scaling, such as medium-sized caching layers, relational databases with low concurrency, and memory-intensive text processing or light language model text generation.
description: Microsoft Azure E8-4as_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. It lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% in stress-ng tests, though multi-core performance and memory write bandwidth are weak, falling in the bottom 25%. The instance exhibits average performance in large-block memory reads and single-threaded compression tasks. Given the high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16 GB per core, the E8-4as_v5 is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive workloads with moderate CPU demands, such as caching layers, text processing, and databases with low parallel processing requirements.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8-4as_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized VM featuring 4 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7763 CPU. It delivers strong single-core performance but weak multi-core scaling.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8-4as_v5 features 4 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC CPU, delivering strong single-core and weak multi-core performance.
tagline: An AMD-powered virtual machine offering high memory density and strong single-core performance for memory-bound, low-concurrency workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor at 3.525 GHz with 4 dedicated vCPUs.
Equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 16:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio.
Delivers strong single-core performance but exhibits weak multi-core scaling.
No local storage or GPU included, with support for external Premium Storage.
Best suited for memory-intensive workloads, caching layers, and low-concurrency databases.
